caption:
SEND this to your favourite coffee lover!

You can thank us later 😉

For the ultimate coffee experience, you need this spot on your Tokyo itinerary. Heck it’s worth booking a Japan trip just to come here!

📌 Koffee Mameya Kakeru 
(their Kiyosumi Shirakawa location, not the one in Shibuya as they have different concepts) 

You can choose one of their 3 omakase menus. Or go a la carte (their espresso martini and cheesecake are supposed to be *amazing*)

💰 Prices start at ¥6,500 and go up to ¥10,000 ($42-65) for the tasting menu so it’s not a cheap experience for our fellow mid-budget travellers. But, as coffee lovers, we thought it was worth every penny

The baristas seriously know what they’re about and their coffee/food pairing were sensational 

📢 Just make sure to book in advance as they don’t have many seats 

——

What to do in Tokyo | Tokyo cafes | Tokyo coffee shops | Japanese coffee culture | Where to eat in Tokyo | Unique things to do in Tokyo | Bougie backpacking | Travel in your 30s | Specialty coffee | Panama geisha coffee | #tokyorestaurant #travelfoodie #ineedcoffee #specialtycoffee #japantrip #koffeemameya
transcript: Well, coffee is now ruined forever. This is the most exciting coffee experience in Japan. In fact, probably even the world. The baristas come mad scientists craft an omakase menu pairing cups of coffee with handmade snacks from around Japan. They have a seasonal, regular and cocktail version. Hello, espresso martinis. And we went all in with their seasonal tasting menu. Honestly, I didn't know coffee and little treats would work so well together. But I'm fully through the bougie looking glass now. And Biscoff just ain't going to cut it anymore. The baristas speak English, so don't worry if your Japanese doesn't extend beyond their passion for coffee is infectious. And they gave us so many recommendations for locally loved spots to add to our Tokyo itinerary, which made the luxury price tag just a little easier to swallow. If you want to change your life through caffeine, you'll find the location and details in the caption.

transcript_segments.text: Well, coffee is now ruined forever.
This is the most exciting coffee experience in Japan.
In fact, probably even the world.
The baristas come mad scientists craft an omakase menu pairing cups of coffee
with handmade snacks from around Japan.
They have a seasonal, regular and cocktail version.
And we went all in with their seasonal tasting menu.
Honestly, I didn't know coffee and little treats would work so well together.
But I'm fully through the bougie looking glass now.
And Biscoff just ain't going to cut it anymore.
The baristas speak English, so don't worry if your Japanese doesn't extend beyond
their passion for coffee is infectious.
And they gave us so many recommendations for locally loved spots to add to our Tokyo itinerary,
which made the luxury price tag just a little easier to swallow.
If you want to change your life through caffeine, you'll find the location and details in the caption.
